comments: For nostalgia fans, the follow-on BBC news has been left on after the midnight pips.
Last 20 minutes of the show are missing in the split-show files.

The headlines, the Tehran hostages are to get a new jailer
The Iranian foreign minister
The Irish group who murdered Airy Neve say they bombed army barracks on Salisbury Plain.
A ton of cannabis is seized in Glasgow.

The Iranian foreign minister, Sadegh Godbazek, has issued a statement saying that he's going to the United States Embassy in Tehran during the day to take charge of the American hostages and the building.
Mr. Godbazek will be acting as the representative of the ruling Revolutionary Council.
The statement made no mention of the condition made by the militant students who took over the embassy at the beginning of last November
that they will hand over the hostages as long as they are allowed first to meet members of the Revolutionary Council
and to appear on television to state their case.
A spokesman for the students has said they're studying Mr. Godzadek's statement and they'll give their reply shortly.
The terrorist group who claimed responsibility for the murder of Airy Neve, the Irish National Liberation Army,
have said they planted the two bombs that went off at an army base on Salisbury Plain.
but so far the claim hasn't been confirmed by the authorities.
Both devices exploded at a training camp for NCOs at Netheraven
run by the School of Infantry.
Two firemen were slightly hurt fighting the fire
caused by the first explosion.
The details from Paul Cannon.
Civil and military police are being assisted in the investigation
by members of Scotland Yard's anti-terrorist squad.
Bomb experts say it looks as if the damage
was caused by two five-pound explosive devices.
They went off within half an hour of each other
at about four o'clock this morning but it wasn't until tonight that the police confirmed that
explosives were in fact to blame. At one stage during the day there were even suggestions that
it was a gas leak. One of the bombs was planted against the wall of a hut where four NCOs were
sleeping. It started a fire and while this was being dealt with there was another explosion in
an adjoining hut also occupied by four NCOs. A soldier in Northern Ireland has been injured by
car bomb is in a very serious condition. His private Sean George Walker, aged 18, of the
King's Own Royal Borderers Regiment. He suffered burns and other injuries when the device exploded
in a crowded street across McGlen in South Armagh. The Provisional IRA have claimed responsibility.
In Rhodesia, Ian Smith has appealed to the white minority not to leave the country in haste,
but to wait and see what life is like under Robert Mugabe. He said that if the Mugabe
government lived up to its promises, there was hope for the future. The Prime Minister-designate
is expected to announce in the morning which posts he's offering to his old Patriotic Front
ally, Joshua Nkomo. From Salisbury, John Thorne. It's now been decided between Mr Mugabe and Mr
Nkomo what jobs the Patriotic Front party, with 20 seats in Parliament, will have. The two leaders
met again today, and it appears that the question of the largely ceremonial position of National
president still figured in the discussions about a future role for Mr. Nkomo. At the same time,
Mr. Mugabe and his party's central committee advisors have decided to complete their cabinet
list over the weekend, and it will be submitted to the British governor, Lord Soames, by Tuesday.
There's been a bomb attack on the Soviet consulate in West Berlin. No one was hurt,
but the building, which is in the American sector, was extensively damaged. An anonymous caller
claimed that Afghan rebels were responsible. Part of the tanker, which broke in two in the
channel has now sunk. It's the forward section and it's feared that four crewmen who are still
missing were trapped inside. 31 other members of the crew were rescued and four bodies were
recovered. The tanker, the Tanyo, which is registered in the Malagasy Republic, broke up
some 40 miles from the French coast. She was carrying 26,000 tons of fuel oil. Customs officers
